title: Silene weberbaueri
taxonomy: Silene weberbaueri is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae. This plant belongs to the genus Silene, which comprises approximately 960 species. Silene weberbaueri is native to Peru, and its classification is further grouped under the kingdom Plantae and the division Magnoliophyta. The plant's classification also includes the class Magnoliopsida and the order Caryophyllales. Silene weberbaueri's classification is a critical aspect of understanding its characteristics, habits, and distribution.

Research has indicated that extracts from Silene weberbaueri may have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. intro: Silene weberbaueri, also known as Weberbauer's catchfly, is a plant species belonging to the family Caryophyllaceae, characterized by its bright pink flowers and unique medicinal properties. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which may help to reduce inflammation and protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Silene weberbaueri include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditional herbal preparations made from Silene weberbaueri include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often used to treat various health issues, including fever, digestive problems, and respiratory infections.
